sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the back of his heel typically after a workout or when he first gets up in the morning, describing it as really hurting in the back of his heel all the way up into his calf. He also reports that when he first gets out of bed, it feels really tight and almost like it's tearing. He reports pain in the left hand and tightness in his leg. The patient reports soreness in his leg. He describes a sensation of tearing in his leg.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's blood pressure is mildly low. His heart rate is within normal limits. A Thompson test is negative. The doctor observes that the patient's left leg hurts a little when he pulls his toes up towards him and pushes against the doctor. The doctor observes that the patient's right leg hurts a little when he pushes against him. The patient's feet are dangling over the edge of the stretcher.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The doctor diagnoses Achilles tendinitis. The doctor notes that the patient's symptoms are most consistent with overuse and recommends stopping training for two weeks. The doctor orders physical therapy visits to address stretching and strengthening.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor recommends taking ibuprofen three tablets every six hours. The doctor orders a follow-up appointment within one week to ten days after the first physical therapy appointment. The doctor will discharge the patient and schedule a follow-up appointment in 7 to 10 days.
